title: Cyclical fever in a 29-year-old returning from Africa
presentation: A 29-year-old male returns from a 3-week trip to sub-Saharan Africa and presents with 5 days of cyclical fever occurring every 48 hours, rigors, headache, and myalgia. He did not take malaria prophylaxis. Vitals: BP 110/72, HR 112, Temp 40.1C, RR 18, SpO2 98%. Exam: splenomegaly, mild jaundice, pallor, no rash, no neck stiffness.

immediate: thick and thin blood smear x3
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Travel to endemic area + cyclical fever + splenomegaly = malaria until proven otherwise. Thick blood smear is gold standard. P. falciparum = IV artesunate (can be fatal). Check glucose — hypoglycemia is common. G6PD before primaquine.
